medical_history.chief_complaint: vesicular rash on both forearms occurring after 2 days of continued sun exposure
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: recurrent vesicular eruptions localized to his nose, cheeks, the rim of his ears, dorsum of hands and forearms related to midday sun exposure of 1-3 h duration each time; itching and a prickly sensation 4-5 h later on the sun-exposed areas, followed by blister formation; healing within 1-2 weeks with scar formation
medical_history.family_history: negative for atopy, blistering diseases and photosensitivity

physical_examination.general_examination: superficial erosions with crusts on the rim of his ears and the dorsum of his hands; vesicles seen on several occasions on the ears, face, extensor aspects of the forearms, and lips; hypertrophic scars present on his hands, and depressed scars on his face

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: culture of blister fluid for herpes simplex virus

other_tests.findings: confluent epidermal necrosis, necrosis of vessel walls and microthrombi within the vessels of some upper dermal vessels
dense superficial and deep perivascular and periadnexal lymphocytic infiltrate, with occasional eosinophils and neutrophils
normal, with minimal erythema dose (MED) to UVA of 75 J cm^-2 (normal, 88 ± 43) and MED to UVB of 60 mJ cm^-2 (normal, 79 ± 27)

other_tests.procedure_name: phototesting with Dermaray for UVB and Mutzhas for UVA
other_tests.findings: decreased MED to UVB (40 mJ cm^-2; normal, 163 ± 50) and decreased MED to UVA (50 mJ cm^-2; normal, 148 ± 32)

sharply demarcated area of epidermal necrosis around a follicular opening, with an intraepidermal blister below it, containing serous fluid and a few neutrophils. Superficial and deep perivascular lymphohistiocytic infiltrate was seen, and scar tissue was present in the mid-reticular dermis

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on typical clinical features, supported by histological findings and negative laboratory investigations. The patient presented with recurrent vesicular eruptions related to sun exposure, healing with scar formation. Histological examination showed confluent epidermal necrosis and necrotizing vasculitis, with no evidence of lupus erythematosus or herpes simplex infection.
